mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes. It leverages Kubernetes custom resources to automate configuration and management tasks, making it easier to set up monitoring for your applications. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and the potential complexity of managing multiple monitoring components.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Prometheus community Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the kube-prometheus-stack.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Prometheus Helm charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the kube-prometheus-stack
steps.description:
This command installs the kube-prometheus-stack Helm chart in the 'monitoring' namespace, which includes Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana.

```bash

kubectl create namespace monitoring

helm install kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.77.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the monitoring stack.
> Installs the kube-prometheus-stack with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Prometheus and Alertmanager pods are running correctly in the monitoring namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Lists all pods in the monitoring namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Access the Prometheus UI
steps.description:
This command sets up port forwarding to access the Prometheus UI from your local machine.

```bash

kubectl port-forward svc/kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus 9090:9090 -n monitoring

echo 'Access Prometheus at http://localhost:9090'

```

> Forwards local port 9090 to the Prometheus service.
> Informs the user where to access the Prometheus UI.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the kube-prometheus-stack
uninstall.description:
This command removes the kube-prometheus-stack and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data.

```bash

helm uninstall kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> Uninstalls the Helm release.
> Deletes the monitoring namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the kube-prometheus-stack
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Prometheus data. This step upgrades the kube-prometheus-stack to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp monitoring/kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus-0:/prometheus /path/to/backup

helm upgrade kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.77.0

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backs up Prometheus data.
> Upgrades the kube-prometheus-stack to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Prometheus" in version "monitoring.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) for Prometheus are not installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:8080: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to incorrect kubeconfig settings.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure you are connected to the correct cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus UI shows 'No data found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if Prometheus is not scraping any targets due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service monitors and scrape configurations in your Prometheus custom resource.

troubleshooting.title: Alertmanager is not sending alerts
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if Alertmanager configuration is incorrect or if it cannot reach the notification endpoint.

**Fix:**
Check the Alertmanager configuration and ensure that the notification channels are correctly set up.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy service monitors.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.76, review the new alerting rules format in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Prometheus UI with metrics being collected and displayed correctly, and the command 'kubectl get pods -n monitoring' should show all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
